title: Family Selector Parity And Dual-Filter Demo Alignment
objective: After TMT discovery is responsive, bring the TMT and EMT demo selector surfaces into source-faithful browse parity by replacing raw or id-driven controls with named dropdowns and paired filter flows that cross-filter trip-unit and breaker or frame selections where the bounded route contracts actually support them.
dependency_note: Do not begin selector parity work until packet 012 proves TMT facets and frames are responsive. Packet 013 must also remain bounded by NETA_EMT_CONTRACT_SPEC.md: it may use the EMT frame search and section flow as the breaker-side selector path, but it must not invent unsupported EMT hardware semantics beyond that contract.
required_inputs: Packet 012 closure evidence proving `/api/v1/neta/tmt/facets` and `/api/v1/neta/tmt/frames` are usable
The current local demo checkpoint in demo/neta_tcc.html where EMT still shows raw manufacturer or trip inputs and TMT is the only family with partial breaker selection
DLL_END_TO_END_MAPPING.md sections for TMT browse and breaker-style matching
NETA_EMT_CONTRACT_SPEC.md describing the bounded EMT frame, context, section-settings, and plot scope
The already-open EasyPower reference UI used for browse-model comparison
constrained_outputs: Named dropdown or paired selector surfaces for TMT and EMT in the local demo
Cross-filter behavior that keeps TMT trip-unit and breaker choices mutually constraining where the route data supports it
An EMT browse flow that resolves manufacturer names and uses bounded frame-plus-section selection without pretending EMT has a broader contract than it actually does
Focused regression coverage or demo-level validation for the selector flows
A dated closure handoff under ops/agents/handoffs
validation_steps: Replace raw ids or free-text-only browse inputs with named selectors where the route contract already provides stable option lists.
Ensure TMT supports paired trip-unit and breaker-style filtering rather than one-sided discovery only.
Ensure EMT resolves manufacturer names and exposes a bounded paired browse flow using the authorized frame and section surfaces instead of raw ids.
Use focused demo tests, route probes, or browser checks to prove the selector flows work after the changes.
Record any remaining EMT differences from EasyPower explicitly as intentional bounded-contract deviations rather than leaving them implicit.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes route, schema-surface, demo, and test updates only. It does not authorize database writes or contract expansion beyond the current TMT and EMT browse surfaces.
closure_note: Closure handoff recorded selector-parity completion on 2026-04-25: the TMT panel replaced five free-text browse inputs with facet-backed selectors, the EMT panel replaced raw manufacturer-id, trip-char, trip-plug, and `q` inputs with six facet-backed named selectors, every selector change now re-fetches `/tmt/facets` or `/emt/facets` with current filters for mutual narrowing, 10 focused tests passed, and live narrowing was verified against the route stack.
